tierDesc.restricted: Your campaign start date must be at least 14 days after payment confirmation.
tierDesc.neutral: Your campaign start date must be at least 7 days after payment confirmation.
tierDesc.trusted: Your campaign start date must be at least 3 days after payment confirmation.
tierDesc.verified: No minimum gap required between payment and campaign start date.

onboarding.title: Account setup
onboarding.subtitle: Confirm your age and choose your account type to continue.
onboarding.betaNoticeText: nx8up is currently in a testing phase and is free to use. Pricing may be introduced once we officially launch.
onboarding.dobHint: You must be 18 or older to use nx8up.
onboarding.creatorDesc: Work with sponsors on campaigns and deals.
onboarding.termsSubtitle: Please read before finishing account setup.
onboarding.termsAgreeCheckbox: I have read and agree to these terms and conditions.
onboarding.termsIntro: These terms summarize how we expect you to use nx8up. They are not a substitute for professional legal advice; your counsel should review them before you rely on them in production.
onboarding.termsEligibilityText: You confirm you are at least 18 years old. You agree to provide accurate information and to keep your login credentials secure. You are responsible for activity under your account.
onboarding.termsCreatorsSponsorsText: You will use the platform in good faith. Creators and sponsors are expected to honor commitments they make through campaigns and communications, comply with applicable laws (including advertising and disclosure rules), and respect others’ rights—including intellectual property and privacy.
onboarding.termsContentText: You retain rights to content you submit, but grant nx8up a limited license to host, display, and operate the service with that content as reasonably needed for the product.
onboarding.termsDisclaimersText: The service is provided “as is” to the maximum extent permitted by law. To the extent permitted, nx8up is not liable for indirect or consequential damages arising from your use of the platform.
onboarding.termsChangesText: We may update these terms; continued use after changes constitutes acceptance of the revised terms where permitted by law. For questions, contact us through the channels listed on the site.

guide.heading: Creator
guide.title: Creator Guide
guide.subtitle: Everything you need to know about using nx8up as a creator.
guide.overviewIntro: nx8up is a creator-first platform connecting gaming content creators with sponsors. As a creator, you apply to sponsored campaigns, negotiate deals, complete deliverables, and get paid — all in one place.
guide.overviewS1Text: Sponsors post campaigns with budgets and requirements. You browse open campaigns, submit applications, and if accepted, enter the Deal Room to finalize terms. Once you complete your deliverables, your payout is processed through Stripe.
guide.overviewS2Text: The dashboard is your command center. It shows your active missions, campaign pipeline, a calendar for scheduling deliverables, and a featured Academy lesson. Everything you need is one click away from the sidebar.
guide.overviewS3Text: You earn XP by completing missions and campaigns. XP levels you up through ranks, which increases your visibility to sponsors and unlocks higher-value campaign opportunities.
guide.profileIntro: A complete profile is your pitch to sponsors. It tells them who you are, what platforms you create on, and what kind of content you make. Profiles with connected platforms rank higher in sponsor searches.
guide.profileS1Text: Go to Profile in the sidebar. Add your bio, select your content categories, and set your primary platform. A complete bio helps sponsors understand your brand and decide if you fit their campaign.
guide.profileS2Text: From your Profile page, click "Connect Twitch." You will be redirected to Twitch to authorize nx8up. Once connected, your follower count and average VOD views sync automatically. This data is shown to sponsors when they review your application.
guide.profileS3Text: Click "Connect YouTube" on the Profile page to authorize access. Your subscriber count and channel name will appear in your profile. Keep your platforms connected — sponsors filter by platform and require up-to-date stats.
guide.profileS4Text: If your content is gaming-focused, use Steam Lookup in the sidebar to verify a Steam account. This shows sponsors your gaming history and adds credibility to game-specific campaign applications.
guide.campaignsIntro: Campaigns are the core of nx8up. Sponsors post campaigns with specific requirements, budgets, and timelines. You browse them, apply to the ones that fit your channel, and wait for a decision.
guide.campaignsS1Text: Click "Campaigns" in the sidebar to see all live campaigns. Each card shows the sponsor, budget, platform requirements, and content type. Read the full campaign details before applying — sponsors can see how thoroughly you read their brief.
guide.campaignsS2Text: Open a campaign and click "Apply." Your profile stats are submitted automatically. Some campaigns may ask additional questions. After submitting, your application status shows as "Pending" on your dashboard.
guide.campaignsS3Text: Pending: your application is under review. Accepted: the sponsor wants to work with you — check the Deal Room. Rejected: the sponsor passed this time. You can apply to other campaigns immediately. Payout due: your campaign deliverables are complete and payment is being processed.
guide.campaignsS4Text: Sponsors can send you direct campaign invites. These appear in your Campaigns tab separately and do not require you to browse for them. Direct invites typically mean the sponsor specifically selected you based on your profile.
guide.dealRoomTitle: Submitting content in the Deal Room
guide.dealRoomIntro: The Deal Room is where you deliver on the campaigns you've been accepted for. Each accepted deal has its own page with the brief, your tracking link, the required deliverables, and the form you use to submit your published content for review. There's no back-and-forth messaging here — it's your submission workspace for completing the deal and getting paid.
guide.dealRoomS1Text: Once a sponsor accepts your application and the campaign launches, the deal appears under "Deal Room" in the sidebar. Each card shows the campaign, your payout, the delivery deadline, and your current submission status. Click a card to open its deal page.
guide.dealRoomS2Title: Review the brief and grab your link
guide.dealRoomS2Text: The deal page lays out the mission requirements (how many videos, streams, posts, or shorts to deliver), the creative package (brand details, content guidelines, and must-include items), and your personal tracking link to include in your content. Read the FTC disclosure reminder — the required paid-promotion tags are mandatory.
guide.dealRoomS3Text: Once your content is live, use the submission form to add each post URL — YouTube and Twitch links are verified against your connected account and auto-fill the publish time — confirm the FTC disclosure, and submit. Your status moves from Pending Review to Verified to Approved, and your payout is released after approval. If a reviewer or the sponsor requests a revision or rejects the submission, their notes appear on the form so you can fix and resubmit.
guide.missionsIntro: Missions are daily and weekly tasks that reward XP. XP builds your rank, which directly affects how often you appear in sponsor searches and how many campaigns you can apply to simultaneously.
guide.missionsS1Text: Missions range from platform activities (stream for 2 hours, post a video) to platform-level actions (apply to a campaign, complete a deal). Each mission shows its XP reward. Completed missions are marked automatically once the platform data syncs.
guide.missionsS2Text: XP accumulates across all completed missions and campaigns. Your level and rank are shown on your dashboard and your public profile. Higher ranks unlock priority placement in sponsor searches.
guide.missionsS3Text: You start as a Recruit and advance through ranks as you earn XP. Each rank tier corresponds to a range of campaign budgets you are most likely to qualify for. Consistent activity keeps your rank climbing.
guide.missionsS4Text: Use the dashboard calendar to log personal tasks and track campaign start/end dates. Campaign events appear automatically on the calendar when your applications are accepted. Add notes to specific days to stay organized.
guide.academyIntro: The Academy is a library of video lessons on growing your channel, building your brand, and creating content that sponsors want. Lessons are free and accessible at any time from the sidebar.
guide.academyS1Text: Click "Academy" in the sidebar to see all available lessons. Each lesson is categorized by topic — Growth, Branding, YouTube, and more. A random featured lesson appears on your dashboard each session.
guide.academyS2Text: Open a lesson to watch the video and follow along with the step-by-step breakdown below the player. Lessons are designed to be actionable — each step gives you something concrete to apply to your channel.
guide.academyS3Text: Topics include: how to grow a gaming channel from scratch, building a recognizable personal brand, optimizing your YouTube channel, understanding analytics, and creating content that attracts sponsors.
guide.payoutsIntro: Payouts are processed through Stripe once a campaign is complete and your deliverables are verified. Make sure your payout method is set up before a campaign ends.
guide.payoutsS1Text: After you complete your campaign deliverables, the sponsor marks the campaign complete. nx8up verifies the deliverables and initiates a payout to your connected Stripe account. The platform fee is deducted automatically.
guide.payoutsS2Text: Go to your Profile and connect a Stripe account for payouts. You will need to complete Stripe's identity verification before your first payout can be processed. This is a one-time setup.
guide.payoutsS3Text: Payouts typically process within 3–5 business days after verification. You can check the status of any pending payout from the Campaigns tab under "Payout due." If a payout is delayed, contact support.
guide.payoutsS4Text: nx8up charges a platform fee on each completed deal. The fee is shown on the campaign details page before you apply, so you always know your net payout in advance.

guide.heading: Sponsor
guide.title: Sponsor Guide
guide.subtitle: Everything you need to know about using nx8up as a sponsor.
guide.overviewIntro: nx8up connects sponsors with gaming content creators. You post campaigns, review creator applications, negotiate deals, and pay creators — all through a single platform with built-in reputation tracking and fraud protection.
guide.overviewS1Text: You create a campaign with your budget, content requirements, and timeline. Creators browse and apply. You review their applications, accept the ones that fit, negotiate deliverables in the Deal Room, and pay on completion. Every step is tracked on your dashboard.
guide.overviewS2Text: The sponsor dashboard shows your KPIs (live campaigns, total budget, accepted creators, acceptance rate), a quick-create button for new campaigns, your current campaign list, and a panel of creators that match your profile settings.
guide.overviewS3Text: Your account has a reputation tier: Neutral, Trusted, or Verified (and Restricted/Sanctioned for policy violations). Higher tiers unlock access to top-tier creators and priority placement. Reputation is built by paying on time, communicating clearly, and completing campaigns.
guide.profileIntro: Your profile tells creators who you are and helps the platform match you with relevant creators. A complete profile with clear targeting criteria produces better matches and more qualified applications.
guide.profileS1Text: Go to Profile in the sidebar. Add your company name and location. These appear on every campaign you post, so creators know who they are applying to work with.
guide.profileS2Text: Set your preferred platforms (Twitch, YouTube, etc.), content types, and game categories. These are used to filter the creator directory and surface matched creators on your dashboard. The more specific you are, the better your matches.
guide.profileS3Text: Set a budget minimum and maximum to indicate the typical deal size for your campaigns. This is not binding — individual campaigns have their own budgets — but it helps creators self-select before applying. Note: ACH payments have a per-transaction limit enforced by Stripe.
guide.profileS4Text: Specify minimum average viewers, subscribers/followers, and engagement rate. Creators who do not meet these thresholds are deprioritized in your matched creators panel. You can still accept any creator you want — these are filters, not hard blocks.
guide.profileS5Text: If your brand promotes age-restricted content (alcohol, gambling, etc.), submit an age restriction request from your Profile page. An admin reviews and approves these requests. You cannot run age-restricted campaigns without approval.
guide.campaignsIntro: Campaigns are how you reach creators. Each campaign defines what you want created, what you will pay, and who you want to create it. Well-written campaigns with clear requirements attract better applications.
guide.campaignsS1Text: Click "New Campaign" from the dashboard or Campaigns page. Fill in the title, description, target platforms, content type, game category, budget, start date, and end date. The description is your brief — be specific about deliverables so creators know exactly what you expect.
guide.campaignsS2Text: Draft: not visible to creators yet. Live: open for applications. Active: deals are in progress. Completed: all deliverables done and payouts sent. Cancelled: campaign was withdrawn. You can edit a campaign while it is in Draft or Live status.
guide.campaignsS3Text: Instead of waiting for applications, you can invite specific creators directly to a campaign. Browse the creator directory, open a creator's profile, and send a direct invite. Invited creators see your campaign immediately in their Campaigns tab.
guide.campaignsS4Text: Live campaigns are visible to all creators who meet your platform and content type requirements. Campaigns marked as direct-invite-only are hidden from the general browse list and only visible to the creators you invite.
guide.creatorsIntro: The Creators directory lets you browse all creators on the platform, filter by platform, size, and content type, and view their full stats before reaching out.
guide.creatorsS1Text: Click "Creators" in the sidebar. The directory lists all active, non-deleted creator profiles. Each card shows their platforms, follower/subscriber counts, average viewers, and engagement rate.
guide.creatorsS2Text: Your dashboard shows a "Matched Creators" panel populated using your profile targeting settings. These are creators who best match your preferred platforms, content type, and creator size requirements. Update your profile to refresh these matches.
guide.creatorsS3Text: Click any creator to see their full profile: bio, linked platforms, platform stats, content categories, and reputation. Stats are synced from Twitch and YouTube automatically — you are always seeing live data.
guide.creatorsS4Text: From a creator's profile page, click "Invite to Campaign" and select which of your live campaigns to invite them to. The creator receives a notification and can accept or decline the invitation.
guide.applicationsIntro: When creators apply to your campaigns, their applications appear in your campaign's Applications tab. You review their profile stats and decide whether to accept or reject.
guide.applicationsS1Text: Go to Campaigns → select a campaign → click Applications. Each applicant's card shows their platform stats, follower count, average viewers, and engagement rate at the time of application.
guide.applicationsS2Text: Click "Accept" on an application to move it to the Deal Room. The creator is notified immediately. You can accept multiple creators per campaign if your budget allows for more than one partnership.
guide.applicationsS3Text: Click "Reject" to decline an application. The creator is notified that this particular campaign was not a match. Rejected creators can still apply to your future campaigns.
guide.applicationsS4Text: Accepted applications move to the Deal Room where you and the creator finalize deliverable specifics. The campaign only becomes active once the deal is confirmed by both parties.
guide.dealRoomTitle: Reviewing submissions in the Deal Room
guide.dealRoomIntro: The Deal Room is where you review the content your accepted creators deliver. Each accepted application has its own page showing the creator's submitted proof, link performance, and the campaign brief — with Approve and Request Revision controls. There's no messaging here; it's your review-and-approval workspace for releasing creator payouts.
guide.dealRoomS1Text: Click "Deal Room" in the sidebar to see every accepted creator for your campaigns. Each card shows the campaign, the creator, the payout, the deadline, and a status badge — Awaiting Submission, Under Admin Review, Needs Review, Revision Requested, or Approved. Click a card to open that creator's deal.
guide.dealRoomS2Text: After the creator submits and our team verifies the proof, the status becomes "Needs Review" and the deal is ready for you. You'll see the published post URLs, an optional screenshot, the post timestamp, and whether the FTC disclosure was confirmed — alongside link performance (clicks, CTR, average video views), the mission requirements, and the creative package.
guide.dealRoomS3Text: If the content meets the brief, click Approve — this releases the creator's payout. If something needs fixing, click Request Revision and add notes explaining what to change; the submission goes back to the creator to fix and resubmit. Once approved, the payout status shows here, and you can retry it if a transfer fails.
guide.paymentsIntro: Payments are processed through Stripe. You pay creators after their deliverables are verified. nx8up deducts a platform fee automatically and handles the payout to the creator.
guide.paymentsS1Text: After a campaign completes and deliverables are verified, you initiate payment from the campaign page. Funds are held by Stripe and released to the creator once the platform confirms completion. The creator receives their portion after the platform fee is deducted.
guide.paymentsS2Text: nx8up supports card payments and ACH bank transfers. Set your preferred payment method in your Profile under "Payment Method." ACH has a per-transaction limit — campaigns with budgets above this limit must be split or paid by card.
guide.paymentsS3Text: A percentage-based platform fee is charged on each completed deal. The exact rate is shown on the campaign creation page and the payment confirmation screen. There are no hidden charges — the fee is always visible before you commit.
guide.paymentsS4Text: Click "Payouts" in the sidebar to see a full history of all completed payments, including creator name, campaign, amount paid, and payout date. You can export this data for accounting purposes.
guide.paymentsS5Text: If a creator fails to deliver or violates campaign terms, you can submit a refund request from the campaign page. An admin reviews the request and mediates the dispute. Decisions are final and communicated to both parties within 5 business days.
